issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle CSS classes and body scroll locks but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the hamburger trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1461: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }` Line 1478: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing period buttons switch between monthly and annual rates but do not update the aria-pressed state. Users of assistive technology will not know which billing cycle is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates the visual active class for billing period buttons but fails to update the aria-pressed attribute. Users of assistive technology will not know which billing period is selected.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override for .btn:active

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, mobileNav.close, and mobileNav.toggle functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users are not informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); In mobileNav.close, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); In mobileNav.toggle, update accordingly.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels without setting aria-expanded on the associated trigger buttons. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive the expanded state of the dropdown, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: billing toggle switch missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The month/year toggle switch (data-billing-toggle) changes state without updating aria-pressed. Screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the switch is in the “annual” or “monthly” position, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _onToggleClick, after setting the period, call: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(period === 'year'));

issuesFound.title: product filter results count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is displayed visually but not announced to screen readers. Users who rely on assistive technology will not know the new result count, degrading the experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates prices visually but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen will not know the billing period has changed, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, after _syncToggleState, add: liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label.toLowerCase()} billing`);

issuesFound.title: form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the error message is displayed but aria-invalid is not set to "true" on the input. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, inside the else block (invalid), add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); In the valid block, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen‑reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of the menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after _trigger is assigned, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded value. Screen‑reader users are not informed that the dropdowns start in a collapsed state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register, after setting aria-haspopup, add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-controls pointing to the drawer it controls. Screen‑reader users cannot understand the relationship between the button and the drawer, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after _drawer is assigned, add: const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree because aria-hidden is not set. Screen‑reader users may still encounter the hidden content, causing confusion (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close, after removing the open class, add: setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); In mobileNav.open, add: setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1760: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1769: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function updates visual state for period toggle buttons but does not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing option is selected, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle button groups.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2503: inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function changes visible prices and savings messages but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on auditory feedback will not know the billing period has changed, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Switched to annual billing');` (or a conditional message based on period).

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count but does not provide a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed when filtering completes or how many results are shown, violating WCAG 4.1.3 for dynamic content updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2204: inside the filterAnimation.run callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : ${totalMatches} products found);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields with error classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2 for form error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3074: inside the `if (!result.valid)` block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the `else` block, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded initial state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded value on the navigation toggle button. When the page loads with the menu closed, screen readers have no information about the button's state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: after `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing aria-expanded initial state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown triggers. Screen readers are not informed that these menus start collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: after `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden mobile nav drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the navigation drawer by removing a CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when hidden, causing screen readers to potentially still navigate into it, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1769: after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`. In mobileNav.open(), after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden dropdown panels not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide panels via the hidden attribute but do not set aria-hidden. When hidden, panels remain in the accessibility tree, causing navigation confusion for screen reader users (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1897: after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`. Line 1907: after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden search suggestions not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.show() and hide() functions toggle the hidden attribute on the suggestion listbox but do not update aria-hidden. When hidden, the list remains accessible to screen readers, causing navigation issues (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2129: after `_listEl.removeAttribute('hidden');`, add `setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`. Line 2135: after `_listEl.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(_listEl, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel slide changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.goTo() function changes the visible slide but only announces via a live region string that is not tied to a proper aria-live region element. The announcement may not be reliably heard by screen readers, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2950: replace the `liveRegion.announce(...)` call with a dedicated aria-live region update: ensure there is an element with `aria-live="polite"` in the carousel container and set its textContent to the announcement string.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error summary not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm._renderErrorSummary() creates an error list but does not set aria-describedby on the form to point to the summary. Screen reader users may not be directed to the error summary when it appears, violating WCAG 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: after `_formEl.insertBefore(_errorSummaryEl, _formEl.firstChild);`, add `setAttr(_formEl, ARIA.DESCRIBEDBY, _errorSummaryEl.id);`. Clear the attribute when errors are fixed.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button visibility not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._update() function shows/hides the scroll button based on scroll position but does not announce its appearance to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen miss the opportunity to use this navigation aid, a best-practice gap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1800: inside the `if (shouldShow && !_visible)` block, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll to top button available');`.

fpTitles: Dynamically hidden dropdown panels not removed from accessibility tree
Form validation error summary not programmatically associated

issuesFound.title: Search input toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and siteSearch._close functions show/hide the suggestion listbox but do not set aria-expanded on the search input (combobox). This breaks screen-reader awareness of the expandable state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In siteSearch._open, add: _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 3500: In siteSearch._close, add: _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function toggles CSS classes on filter tab buttons but does not set aria-pressed. Screen-reader users cannot perceive which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: In productFilter._syncTabAriaStates, add: btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates CSS classes on period buttons but does not set aria-pressed. This prevents screen-reader users from knowing which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: In pricing._syncToggleState, add: btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, no live region announcement is made. Screen-reader users are not informed of the change in results count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4100: In productFilter.applyFilters, after updating the count, add: liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, price updates are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive tech may not know the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4600: In pricing.setPeriod, after updating prices, add: liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen-reader users are not informed when answers expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5000: In faqAccordion.open, add: liveRegion.announce(`Expanded: ${item.question}`); Line 5000: In faqAccordion.close, add: liveRegion.announce(`Collapsed: ${item.question}`);

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields as invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot programmatically detect invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6000: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when result.valid is false, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); when valid, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen-reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In mobileNav.init, after obtaining _trigger, add: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (combobox) does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen-reader users are not aware that the input controls an expandable suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In siteSearch.init, after obtaining _inputEl, add: _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non-button element without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button click handler is attached via addEventListener but no keydown handler for Enter/Space is provided. Keyboard users cannot activate the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7000: In scrollToTop.init, add: _btn.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onClick(e); });

issuesFound.title: Personal data input missing autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form input for email (name="email") does not have an autocomplete attribute. This hinders users with assistive technology from auto-filling the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 8000: In contactForm.init, after obtaining the email field, add: emailField.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');

issuesFound.title: Progress bar missing ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar element (data-wizard-progress) has no role="progressbar", aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, or aria-valuemax. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the progress.
issuesFound.fix: Line 9000: In multiStepWizard._updateProgress, ensure _progressBar has: role="progressbar", aria-valuemin="0", aria-valuemax="100", and set aria-valuenow dynamically.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not cleared from aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to 'false' or removed. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6000: In inlineValidator._renderResult, when result.valid is true, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav trigger button does not have aria-controls pointing to the navigation drawer's id. Screen-reader users cannot programmatically link the trigger to the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In mobileNav.init, after obtaining _trigger and _drawer, add: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update via innerHTML without live region
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.render function updates the listbox content via innerHTML but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users relying on assistive tech may not know suggestions have loaded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5500: In suggestionListRenderer.render, after updating innerHTML, add: liveRegion.announce(`${items.length} suggestions available`);

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non-interactive element without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger click handler is attached to a button, but the button also has hover handlers that open the dropdown without keyboard support. Keyboard users cannot open the dropdown via hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In dropdownMenu.register, add a keydown handler to the trigger that opens the dropdown on Enter/Space.

issuesFound.title: Missing form label association
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form input for message (textarea) does not have an associated <label> element or aria-labelledby. Screen-reader users may not know the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 8000: In contactForm.init, ensure the textarea has an associated label via <label for="..."> or aria-labelledby.

issuesFound.title: ARIA live region not atomic for dynamic updates
issuesFound.explanation: The live region used for announcements has aria-atomic="false", meaning only changed text is announced. For complete context, atomic should be "true" for some announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2000: In liveRegion.init, set aria-atomic="true" on the polite and assertive wrappers.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element for desktop navigation lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. When multiple navigation landmarks exist on a page (desktop and mobile), screen reader users cannot distinguish between them without unique labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1013: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element inside the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. This creates an unnamed landmark region, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: Carousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the carousel's previous control lacks an aria-label prop. Icon-only interactive elements require an accessible name; otherwise, screen reader users cannot understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1768: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: Carousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the carousel's next control lacks an aria-label prop. Icon-only interactive elements require an accessible name; otherwise, screen reader users cannot understand its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1774: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: Decorative marquee clone list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicate integration logo list used for visual marquee effect does not have aria-hidden="true". This causes screen readers to announce duplicate content, creating a confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element inside PlanCard lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. When multiple similar articles exist, screen reader users cannot distinguish between them without unique accessible names.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1605: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not set aria-busy when isLoading is true. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is in a loading state, which is critical feedback during asynchronous operations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 832: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Active desktop navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in desktop navigation lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which navigation item corresponds to the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1038: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Active mobile navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in mobile navigation lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which navigation item corresponds to the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Non-visible carousel slide not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel slides that are not currently visible do not have aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may still announce hidden content, causing confusion about what is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-hidden={!isVisible}>

fpTitles: Pricing plan card article missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation menu toggle button does not have aria-expanded to reflect the open state. Screen reader users cannot know if the mobile navigation panel is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button does not have aria-expanded to indicate whether the answer panel is open. Screen reader users cannot know the expanded state of the disclosure.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component used for theme toggling does not have an aria-label prop, making it an icon-only button without an accessible name. Screen reader users will not know the purpose of the button.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle active state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons use only visual styling (className) to indicate the active state, without aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the monthly button and aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the annual button.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section does not have an accessible name, making it a generic landmark without a label. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify the purpose of this section.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar section does not have an accessible name, making it a generic landmark without a label. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify the purpose of this section.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicate list of integration logos used for visual looping is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the second ul element.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Slides that are not currently visible are only hidden with CSS, not from assistive technology. Screen reader users may still access content that is not visually presented.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to each slide div.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not set aria-busy when isLoading is true, failing to communicate the busy state to assistive technology. Screen reader users may not know the button is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation does not have aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify the current page within the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the anchor element when item.current is true.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation does not have aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify the current page within the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-current="page" to the anchor element when item.current is true.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the dropdown trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce unnecessary content.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the mobile navigation dropdown trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce unnecessary content.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span element.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The icon inside the ThemeToggle button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce the icon text.
issuesFound.fix: Wrap the icon text in a span with aria-hidden="true".

issuesFound.title: HeroSection play/pause button icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause icon inside the video toggle button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce the icon character.
issuesFound.fix: Wrap the icon text in a span with aria-hidden="true".

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next button icons not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icons inside the previous and next buttons are decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce the icon characters.
issuesFound.fix: Wrap the icon text in a span with aria-hidden="true".

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the FAQ trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce the icon character.
issuesFound.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span element.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input error message not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has an aria-describedby pointing to 'newsletter-email-error', but the error message is not rendered with that id. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup component.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input error message not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The name input has an aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-name-error', but the error message is not rendered with that id. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" and errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup component.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input error message not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has an aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-email-error', but the error message is not rendered with that id. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup component.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input error message not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input has an aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-subject-error', but the error message is not rendered with that id. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-subject-error" and errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup component.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea error message not programmatically associated
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea has an aria-describedby pointing to 'contact-message-error', but the error message is not rendered with that id. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the error.
issuesFound.fix: Pass errorId="contact-message-error" and errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} to the FormGroup component.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button (min-height:28px)
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links and buttons have a minimum height of 28 px, which is smaller than the recommended 44 px touch target, affecting users on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 944: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button (min-height:28px)
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons are only 28 px tall, below the 44 px target size, making them hard to activate by touch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 968: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button (min-height:28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons are 28 px tall, below the WCAG‑recommended 44 px minimum, reducing accessibility for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1074: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .social-links a (min-height:28px)
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑link anchors are only 28 px tall, falling short of the 44 px minimum target size, which can cause difficulty for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 770: `.social-links a { min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: `word-spacing: -0.2em;` applies a negative word‑spacing, which can reduce readability and cause overlapping text for screen‑reader users relying on visual cues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a `transform` transition for the active state, but no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables or overrides this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform & box‑shadow)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image transitions `transform` and `box‑shadow` on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion override for this selector, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: `.feature-card:hover` animates `transform` (translateY) without a reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .feature-card a::after (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The pseudo‑element animates `transform: translateX(3px)` on hover, but no reduced‑motion guard is provided.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card:hover` animates `transform: translateY(-2px)` without a reduced‑motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover (transform & scale)
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card animates `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` on hover, lacking a reduced‑motion guard.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .stat-item:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: `.stat-item:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-2px)` without a reduced‑motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section animates `transform: translateY(-1px)` on hover, with no reduced‑motion override.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions change the visual state of the mobile navigation drawer but never update the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set `aria‑controls` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button that opens the mobile drawer never receives an `aria‑controls` reference to the drawer element, leaving the relationship between control and controlled region undefined for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` adds the “open” class to the panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the associated button, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` removes the open class but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the ARIA state stale.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not reflect state with `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The period‑selection buttons (`[data-period]`) are visually toggled via CSS classes, but the ARIA pressed state is never updated, violating the button‑role pattern.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `setPeriod()` updates the visible price text but does not send a live‑region announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware that the billing period has changed.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on failing fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields with error classes but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error state.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list renderer hides the list with `hidden` but does not set `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.hide()` adds the `hidden` attribute to the `<ul>` but does not also set `aria-hidden="true"`. Elements removed from the accessibility tree should have both attributes to ensure consistent behavior across browsers.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation Escape‑key handler does not reset `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: `_onTriggerKeydown()` listens for Escape to close the drawer but only calls `close()`; the `close()` implementation (see Issue 1) now updates `aria-expanded`, but the current code does not call `close()` with the ARIA update, leaving the attribute unchanged when Escape is used.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `close()` is invoked (which now updates `aria-expanded`) or directly set the attribute in the key handler.

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications are hidden with CSS classes only, without `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When a toast is dismissed, the code removes the visible class and adds a hidden class but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the toast element, so the element may remain perceivable to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not set `aria‑expanded` on the toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds CSS classes to show the off‑canvas drawer but never updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not set `aria‑expanded` on the toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` hides the drawer but never resets `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the state stale for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not set `aria‑expanded` on the toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The generic `toggle()` helper calls `open()`/`close()` but does not ensure `aria‑expanded` is kept in sync when the button is activated via keyboard or click.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `open()` and `close()` (see Issues 1 & 2) always update `aria‑expanded`; no additional code needed after those fixes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened the script adds the `open` class to the panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger element, so screen readers cannot detect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not set `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown removes the `open` class but never resets `aria‑expanded` to `"false"` on the trigger, leaving the state inaccurate.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, so assistive technology cannot announce the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.close(item)` collapses the panel but does not reset `aria‑expanded` to `"false"` on the trigger, leaving the state stale.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not set `aria‑pressed` to reflect selection
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive the visual `CSS.active` class but never receive `aria‑pressed="true"`/`"false"`, which is required for screen‑reader users to know which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle does not set `aria‑pressed` on the switch element
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but its `aria‑pressed` state is never updated when the user switches between monthly and annual billing.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list does not update `aria‑expanded` on the search input
issuesFound.explanation: When the autocomplete panel is opened (`_open()`), the search `<input>` receives `aria‑autocomplete="list"` but never gets `aria‑expanded="true"`. When closed it also never resets the attribute, leaving the state ambiguous for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` function adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the failing field (or clears it on success). This prevents screen readers from announcing validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden mobile navigation drawer is not removed from the accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but the script never toggles `aria‑hidden` on the drawer. When hidden, the drawer remains focusable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false')`; in `close()` set `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Suggestion list renderer does not set `aria‑expanded` on the search input (duplicate of Issue 10) – already covered.

issuesFound.title: IconButton instances missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: Three IconButton components (mobile navigation close button, theme toggle button, and carousel prev/next buttons) are rendered without an aria‑label, title, or visible text, resulting in icon‑only interactive elements with no accessible name.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor (unassociated label)
issuesFound.explanation:
The custom FormGroup component renders a <label> element without an htmlFor attribute, so none of the associated inputs are programmatically linked to their labels, breaking accessibility for screen‑reader users.
  Fix (example for one instance – apply to all):
  Line 619:    <label className="form-label" htmlFor={htmlFor}> // add htmlFor prop
  (Repeat the same change in every FormGroup usage.)

issuesFound.title: TextInput does not set aria‑invalid when invalid
Broken aria‑describedby references on form fields
issuesFound.explanation:
Several inputs set aria‑describedby to IDs such as “newsletter-email-error” or “contact-name-error”, but no element with those IDs exists in the markup, leaving the reference broken.
  Fix (example for newsletter email):
  After the TextInput, add:
  <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />
  (Similarly add matching ErrorMessage components for each referenced ID.)

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual asterisk not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The required‑field marker “*” is rendered as visible text without aria‑hidden, causing screen readers to announce an extraneous asterisk. Fix (apply to each required marker): Replace   * with   *

issuesFound.title: Loading buttons missing aria‑busy
issuesFound.explanation:
Buttons that show a loading state (isSubmitting) do not expose that state via aria‑busy, so assistive technologies cannot know the button is busy.
  Fix (example for newsletter button):
  <Button
    type="submit"
    variant="primary"
    size="md"
    loading={isSubmitting}
    aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
    aria-busy={isSubmitting} // add this attribute
    aria-describedby={statusId}
  >

issuesFound.title: Multiple <nav> elements lack accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains three separate <nav> landmarks without aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, making it impossible for screen‑reader users to differentiate them.

- SkipLinks:  <nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">
  - DesktopNav:  <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation">
  - MobileNav body:  <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: <section> elements missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The hero and stats sections are landmarks without aria‑label or aria‑labelledby, so they lack a programmatic name.
issuesFound.fix:
- HeroSection:  <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">
  - StatsBar:  <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown submenu toggles visibility via the isOpen state but does not expose that state with aria‑expanded. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 306: `<button` → ` <button aria-expanded={isOpen}`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: IconButton requires an aria‑label to give an accessible name for icon‑only buttons. The theme toggle renders only an icon (☀️/🌙/💻) without a label, making it invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896: `<IconButton` → ` <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]}`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is an IconButton containing only the “✕” glyph and has no aria‑label, so it is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 761: `<IconButton` → ` <IconButton aria-label="Close mobile navigation"`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel navigation buttons are IconButton elements that contain only “‹”/“›” characters and lack aria‑label, leaving them without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1155: `<IconButton` → ` <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial"`  
  Fix: Line 1160: `<IconButton` → ` <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial"`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label not associated with input (NewsletterForm email)
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup renders a <label> without an htmlFor attribute, so the email <input> has no programmatic label. This violates WCAG 2.2 §1.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1012: `<label className="form-label">` → ` <label htmlFor="newsletter-email" className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label not associated with input (ContactForm name)
issuesFound.explanation: Same issue as above for the “Full name” field; the <label> lacks htmlFor, leaving the input unlabeled.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1334: `<label className="form-label">` → ` <label htmlFor="contact-name" className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: TextInput does not forward aria‑invalid when invalid (ContactForm name)
issuesFound.explanation: TextInput receives an invalid prop but never maps it to aria‑invalid, so error state is not announced to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1245‑1252: replace the <input> with  
  ` <input aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} … />`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual toggle buttons visually indicate the selected period but do not expose the state via aria‑pressed, preventing screen‑reader users from knowing which option is active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 950: `<button` → ` <button aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}`  
  Fix: Line 954: `<button` → ` <button aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons change appearance when selected but lack aria‑pressed, so the selected filter is not communicated to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 870: `<button` → ` <button aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}`

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation <nav> missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The primary desktop navigation is a <nav> element without an accessible name, and another <nav> exists (mobile nav), violating landmark‑labeling rules.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: `<nav id="main-nav"` → ` <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation"`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation <nav> missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a <nav> without an accessible name, creating duplicate unlabeled landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 782: `<nav className="mobile-nav__body"` → ` <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation"`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks <nav> missing aria‑label
issuesFound.explanation: The skip‑link container is a <nav> with no label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 688: `<nav>` → ` <nav aria-label="Skip links"`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard <article> missing aria‑labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: Each pricing plan card is an <article> without aria‑labelledby referencing its heading, so screen readers receive no name for the region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: `<article className="pricing-card__article">` → ` <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: Required‑field asterisk not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The visual “*” marker for required fields is rendered as visible text without aria‑hidden, causing screen readers to read a stray asterisk.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1242: ` *` → ` *`

issuesFound.title: Submit button loading state not announced via aria‑busy (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> receives loading={isSubmitting} but the component does not set aria‑busy, so AT users are unaware that the button is processing.
issuesFound.fix: In Button component (line 447‑452), add `aria-busy={isLoading}` to the <button> element.

issuesFound.title: Submit button loading state not announced via aria‑busy (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 16 for the contact form’s submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same change to Button component (see Issue 16).

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria‑current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link receives a CSS class nav-link--current but no aria‑current="page", so AT cannot identify the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 744: `<a` → ` <a aria-current="page"`   (add conditionally when item.current is true)

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility broken — content visible to sighted users
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible instead of the required position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip rect. This means content marked as screen-reader-only will be fully visible to sighted users, defeating the purpose of the utility and potentially cluttering the visual interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 479-488: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.fix: Line 933: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, significantly below the 44px recommended minimum. These are especially difficult to activate for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950-951: min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended minimum. Users may have difficulty selecting filter options on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1059: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended minimum. Users may struggle to switch between monthly and annual billing options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, below the 44px recommended minimum. Touch users may have difficulty selecting footer links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1454: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended minimum. Users may struggle to tap social icons on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1476: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with transition: transform var(--transition-base). The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 535 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 535-537: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform with perspective and rotate with transition. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 877-882 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D transform effect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 877-882: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .steps-list > .step + .step::before
issuesFound.explanation: The step connecting line uses transform: translateY(-100%) but has no prefers-reduced-motion override. While this is a decorative element, users who prefer reduced motion should not see this transform applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1118: Add after line 1118: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .steps-list > .step + .step::before { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item:hover rule uses transform: translateY(-2px) with transition. While there is a prefers-reduced-motion override for the hover state, it should also disable the transition property itself to fully respect user preference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280-1283: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Many link styles rely only on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 353: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button uses transform: translateY(-1px) on hover with transition. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists for this selector. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this movement.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 830: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator from all interactive elements for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, leaving users in high-contrast mode without any focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator not restored for inset focus ring utility
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only adjusts outline-offset but does not restore a visible outline style, inheriting outline: none from the global rule.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator color set without restoring outline style
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the global rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Navigation menu links and buttons lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and button:focus-visible selectors set outline: none without providing a visible alternative, leaving keyboard users without focus indication on navigation items.

issuesFound.title: Submenu links lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from dropdown menu links.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button lacks visible focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative, making the search button invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from account links.

issuesFound.title: Feature card links lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative, making card links invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Button component lacks visible focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from all buttons using this class.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative, leaving keyboard users without focus indication on filter controls.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from billing toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper lacks visible focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative, making the scrollable table region invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion buttons lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from expandable FAQ buttons.

issuesFound.title: Form inputs lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, textarea:focus-visible selectors set outline: none without providing a visible alternative, violating WCAG 2.4.7 for form controls.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement, removing focus indication from footer links.

issuesFound.title: Social media links lack visible focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative, making social links invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum, creating a barrier for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size, making checkboxes difficult to tap accurately.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px without sufficient padding compensation, which may be below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, making content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Skip link animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class includes a transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override, which may cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which may trigger vestibular disorders in users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Button animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition without a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable the animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Product card animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card class includes a transform transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which may cause issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 206: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on body impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which reduces legibility by compressing characters together, particularly problematic for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset and border-radius. Without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border, keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Users relying on high-contrast mode lose all focus visibility because no alternative focus indicator is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 270-273: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip links
issuesFound.explanation: .skip-link defines transition: transform var(--transition-base) without a corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable it. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines transitions on transform and includes transform: translateY(1px) on :active, without a reduced-motion override. The empty media query at line 334 does not cover this selector.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA buttons

issuesFound.fix: Add to line 334: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary, .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero images
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img defines transitions on transform and transform on :hover. The empty media query at line 334 does not override these properties, leaving the 3D perspective transform active for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
