issuesFound.fix: Line 688: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are sized at 20px by 20px with a minimum height of 20px, below the 24px minimum target size. This makes selection difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1569: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The base .btn class sets a minimum height of 32px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size for pointer inputs. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these controls reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: .btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); text-align: center; text-decoration: none; white-space: nowrap; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; border: 2px solid transparent; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); vertical-align: middle; position: relative; isolation: isolate; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has minimum dimensions of 28px by 28px, meeting the 24px absolute minimum but below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 755: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links and buttons have a minimum height of 28px, which while meeting the 24px minimum falls short of the 44px recommended target size for reliable pointer activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 782: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have a minimum height of 28px, below the recommended 44px target size, potentially causing activation difficulties for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 813: [id$="-submenu"] li a { display: block; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input field has an explicit height of 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 837: #search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has fixed dimensions of 28px by 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 856: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have a minimum height of 28px, below the recommended 44px target size for reliable touch activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214: .filter-tabs button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background: none; border: none; border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have a minimum height of 28px, below the recommended 44px target size for comfortable pointer interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1354: .pricing-toggle button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-full); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have a minimum height of 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size for reliable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1623: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target meets minimum but below recommended on footer nav
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a minimum height of 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size for optimal accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1604: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class incorrectly defined
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible dimensions, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This renders the utility non-functional, causing screen-reader-only text to be displayed to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 302-312: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query empty
issuesFound.explanation: The prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 348 is empty, failing to disable animations for users who require reduced motion. This can trigger vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform animation missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transform and box-shadow transitions that create spatial movement on hover, but the prefers-reduced-motion media query block for this element is empty, failing to respect user motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 930: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Button transitions missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have transitions affecting transform properties that animate spatial changes, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable these animations for users with motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: (add to existing block) .btn { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Feature card transitions missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards have transform transitions that create spatial movement on hover, but the existing reduced-motion override only disables the hover transform, not the transition itself or other properties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Product card transitions missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards animate with transform transitions that shift the card vertically, but the reduced-motion override only disables the hover state transform without disabling the transition properties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1271: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Stat item transitions missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Statistics items have transform transitions creating spatial movement on hover, but the reduced-motion block only disables the transform without removing the transition properties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1310: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } .stat-item:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transitions missing reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards have transform and scale transitions that create spatial movement, but the reduced-motion override only disables the hover transform without disabling the underlying transitions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1386: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can make content difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause text to overlap at certain zoom levels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 261: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses a light red background (#ee9999) with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.5:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text and 3:1 for large text under WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 672: .btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor element selector removes text underlines without ensuring all links have a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom or font-weight change. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users unless specific hover states are defined.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: Body text size below recommended 16px minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets a base font-size of 12px, causing all rem-based text sizing to render smaller than the recommended 16px minimum for body text. This forces users to zoom to read comfortably, creating a barrier for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 259: font-size: 16px; (or font-size: 100%;)

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that shows and hides a suggestion list, but the aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the open/closed state. Assistive technologies cannot announce whether suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open function): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line (in _close function): setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the code adds error descriptions but never sets aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clears it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
  Line (in markValid, after removing describedby): setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen readers assume the state is unknown, causing confusion before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after _trigger assignment and null check): setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users do not receive feedback about how many products match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after setAttr for aria-label): liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods, the price values update visually with animation but the new prices are not announced. Screen reader users are not informed of the price changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line (after priceDisplay.setAttribute('aria-label', target);): liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} ${priceFormatter.periodLabel(period)}`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is shown and hidden using CSS classes, but aria-hidden is not updated. When closed, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and can be accessed by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function, after addClass(_drawer, ...)): setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line (in close function, after removeClass(_drawer, ...)): setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header is hidden visually when scrolling down but remains accessible to screen readers because aria-hidden is not set. Users can navigate to off-screen header content that is not visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in hide function, after addClass): setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line (in show function, after removeClass): removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');

fpTitles: stickyHeader missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdown menu controller toggle the panel visibility but do not synchronise the aria-expanded state on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1510: After `inst.open = true;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 1523: After `inst.open = false;` add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open() missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the function sets the open state and CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function correctly sets it to false, the asymmetry leaves screen readers unaware when content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3220: After `item.open = true;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) update their visual active state but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which option is currently selected to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2941: Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach()` loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The validation result renderer adds CSS classes and error messages but never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form inputs to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3313: Inside the `if (result.valid)` block, after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);` add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);`
  Line 3318: Inside the `else` block (invalid), after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);` add: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable but never initialises it with createFocusTrap() or activates it in the open() function. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer when open, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close not returning focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer closes, focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1419: At the end of the close() function, after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` add: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss lacks pause control
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout without providing a mechanism to pause, stop, or extend the timer. Users who need more time to read the notification (including screen reader users) cannot prevent it from disappearing.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are registered during init(), the trigger button receives aria-haspopup and aria-controls but not aria-expanded="false". Screen readers announce the button without indicating that it controls a collapsed panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1556: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that expands and collapses a suggestion listbox, but the _open() and _close() functions do not update aria-expanded to reflect the listbox visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2484: After `_searchState.open = true;` add: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line 2497: After `_searchState.open = false;` add: `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge is updated visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware that the filter results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2630: After `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', ...)` add: `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The breadcrumb processor identifies the last item as the current page but never sets aria-current="page" on the link, preventing screen readers from identifying which page in the breadcrumb trail is the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1340: After `const lastLink = qs('a', lastItem);` add: `if (lastLink) lastLink.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid/markValid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function adds error descriptions but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field, and markValid() does not clear it. Screen readers cannot convey the validity state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1238: At the end of markInvalid(), add: `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
  Line 1251: At the end of markValid(), add: `field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

fpTitles: toastManager auto-dismiss lacks pause control

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When `mobileNav.init()` runs, it finds the trigger button (`_trigger`) but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` to reflect the initial closed state of the mobile navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180 (in `init`, after `_trigger` is assigned): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function does not move keyboard focus into the navigation drawer, and the `close` function does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the page when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2130 (in `open`, at end): Add `_drawer.focus();` (or `getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0].focus()`). Line 2150 (in `close`, before `emit`): Add `_trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to update `aria-expanded` on the trigger element (`inst.trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280 (in `_openInstance`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`. Line 2290 (in `_closeInstance`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdowns are registered in `init`, the code sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button to indicate the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250 (in `register`, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')`): Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` expands the FAQ panel and updates CSS classes, but it does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`item.trigger`). The `close` function does set it to `false`, creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120 (in `open`, after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open)`): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_open` and `_close` functions manage the visibility of the search suggestions listbox but do not update `aria-expanded` on the combobox input (`_inputEl`), which is required for the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2780 (in `_open`): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`. Line 2790 (in `_close`): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function sets up the search input as a combobox with `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but fails to initialise `aria-expanded="false"` to reflect that the suggestion list is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850 (in `init`, after setting other ARIA attributes): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed (or aria-checked)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (`[data-period]`) indicate active state via CSS classes only. They do not update `aria-pressed` (for buttons) or `aria-checked` (for the switch role) to communicate the selected state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3540 (in `_syncToggleState`, inside `_periodBtns.forEach`): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` (or `ARIA.CHECKED` if the element has `role="switch"`).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, `_renderResult` calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which links the error message via `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. When valid, `markValid` clears `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="false"`. Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3900 (in `_renderResult`): Replace the `if (result.valid)` block with `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, !result.valid);` or add explicit `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true)` in the invalid branch and `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false)` in the valid branch.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function hides the drawer by removing a CSS class. If the CSS uses `visibility:hidden` or `transform` rather than `display:none`, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree. The code does not set `aria-hidden="true"` when closing (and remove it when opening).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150 (in `close`): Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`. Line 2130 (in `open`): Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (monthly/annual), the price updates visually and the toggle state changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers via the `liveRegion` utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500 (in `setPeriod`, after `_syncToggleState`): Add `liveRegion.announce('Switched to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label + ' billing');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not announce open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions accept an `announceToSR` parameter but do not use it to trigger a live region announcement. Users are not informed when the navigation menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2130 (in `open`): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen);`. Line 2150 (in `close`): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not announce open/close state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden without announcing the state change to screen readers, even though focus may move into the panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280 (in `_openInstance`): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line 2290 (in `_closeInstance`): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible card count updates and the grid re-renders, but the result count is not announced to screen readers (e.g., "Showing 5 products").
issuesFound.fix: Line 4100 (in `applyFilters`, inside `filterAnimation.run` callback): Add `liveRegion.announce('Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initialises or activates a focus trap when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the underlying page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2130 (in `open`): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`. Line 2150 (in `close`): Add `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function does not move focus into the answer panel, and `close` does not return focus to the question trigger. Keyboard users may lose context when expanding/collapsing items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120 (in `open`, at end): Add `item.panel.focus();`. Line 3140 (in `close`, at end): Add `item.trigger.focus();`.

fpTitles: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
productFilter applyFilters does not announce results
faqAccordion missing focus management

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between the label and the input field, meaning screen reader users cannot identify which label belongs to which input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1052: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: TextInput missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the error state of the field to users when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot announce the error state of the textarea to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1108: `<textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot convey the error state of the select field to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1128: `<select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without the required aria-label prop. The button contains only an emoji icon with no accessible name, so screen reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} aria-label={LABELS[theme]} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation panel lacks an aria-label. The button contains only a multiplication symbol with no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: `<IconButton onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation panel" className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: `<IconButton aria-controls={trackId} aria-label="Previous testimonial" onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing aria-label

issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link indicating the current page (marked with item.current) lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1275: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk ( *) is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "star" before the visually hidden "(required)" text, causing redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1056: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an isLoading state but does not set aria-busy. Screen reader users are not notified that the button is processing and temporarily unavailable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1030: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on incorrect element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. The aria-modal attribute should be on the element with role="dialog" to correctly convey modality to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2145: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line 2146: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial slides that are not visible (isVisible === false) lack aria-hidden="true". Screen readers can access all slides simultaneously, making it impossible for users to understand the carousel navigation and causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1940: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders star symbols (★ and ☆) in spans without aria-hidden. These are decorative visual indicators; the rating value is already conveyed via the aria-label on the parent span. Screen readers may announce "black star" or "white star" redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 995: `{filled ? '★' : '☆'}`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon (▲/▼) in the NavDropdown trigger is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. The expanded state is conveyed via aria-expanded, so the arrow symbol is redundant and may be announced as text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`
